_comment: SYNTHETIC example for the public package — a fictional brand, not a real client. It shows the images-mode config shape and the 'every prompt is a complete photographic brief' rule. Prompts here are illustrative, newly written for documentation.
title: klint-launch
brief: Klint Ceramics — matte stoneware pour-over coffee dripper in oatmeal glaze, slow-craft studio positioning. This run: a three-shot launch micro-batch on a single lighting recipe — one hero, one lifestyle, one detail. Daylight studio, calm and warm.

clips.prompt: Hero still of a matte oatmeal-glaze stoneware pour-over coffee dripper on pale oak, three-quarter front, soft north-window daylight from camera-left at 45 degrees (large soft source, fill 2:1), gentle rim separating the rim of the cone from a warm off-white background, 85mm f/2.8, shallow but readable depth, Kodak Portra 400 grade with lifted blacks, in the manner of Irving Penn still-life stillness.

Lifestyle frame of two hands mid-pour with a gooseneck kettle into the same oatmeal stoneware dripper on a linen-draped counter, steam catching the window light, same north-window daylight from camera-left at 45 degrees, 50mm f/2.0 for natural context, deep enough focus to keep both kettle spout and dripper sharp, same Kodak Portra 400 grade, in the manner of Irving Penn warmth.

Detail macro of the dripper's spiral inner ribs and the matte glaze texture meeting the unglazed clay foot, raking north-window daylight from camera-left grazing the ribs to reveal surface, 100mm macro f/4 sharp across the rib field, same Kodak Portra 400 grade, in the manner of Irving Penn material study.
